evidence_inventory.notes: Synthetic example created only to demonstrate the public handoff format.
No customer text, account data, or real seller export is included.

known_facts: Users in the synthetic evidence set value adjustable compartment width.
The available evidence does not include supplier quotes or measured package dimensions.
signal_reading: Adjustability may be a useful product-definition direction, but the mechanism is not yet validated.
risks: Added mechanism complexity may increase cost, defect rate, and package size.

data_gaps.why_it_matters: Stage 006 cannot produce a defensible ROI range without confirmed cost inputs.
data_gaps.next_action: Request comparable quotes using one parameterized product-definition sheet.

data_gaps.why_it_matters: FBA fee sensitivity depends on the packaged size and weight.
data_gaps.next_action: Measure the first sample and rerun the fee model.

data_gaps.why_it_matters: It affects variant planning but does not block the next validation stage.
data_gaps.next_action: Collect public listing and review signals after the structural concept is stable.

decisions_for_meeting: Whether to fund supplier RFQs and one functional sample.
Whether adjustability is a required feature or an optional variant.

required_inputs: Supplier quote with MOQ and lead time
acceptance_checks: Every numeric claim points to an evidence item or is labeled as an estimate.
Product requirements use measurable values, ranges, enums, or explicit pending fields.
No final ROI, compliance, patent, or launch decision appears in the Stage 005 handoff.
